channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire extended by 3 weeks, says Trump
item.description: US President Trump announced a three-week extension to the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire, following a high-level negotiation at the White House. The agreement marks the second such deal between Israel and Lebanon in a week.

item.title: Trump Says Israel and Lebanon Agree to Extend Cease-Fire
item.description: Trump extended the Israel and Lebanon cease-fire for three more weeks to advance a peace agreement in Iran. The president said the truce in Lebanon would last three more weeks.

item.title: Trump orders U.S. military to 'shoot and kill' Iranian boats mining Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US President Trump ordered the military to shoot and kill Iranian boats mining the Strait of Hormuz. The move comes amid escalating tensions between the US and Iran. Live updates: Trump orders U. S.

item.title: Trump announces extension to Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
item.description: US President Trump announced an extension to the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. The agreement, which lasts three weeks, follows a White House meeting between Israeli and Lebanese officials. Iran-US war latest: Trump announces extension to Israel-Lebanon ceasefire The Independent.

item.title: Trump says Israel and Lebanon agree to extend ceasefire by 3 weeks
item.description: Blasts were reported over Tehran, prompting Iran to activate its air defenses. Iran had previously stated it wouldn't reopen the Strait of Hormuz as long as the US blockades its ports.

item.title: Israel and Lebanon agree to extend ceasefire by 3 weeks, Trump says
item.description: Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend their ceasefire by three weeks, US President Trump said. The deal was reached after a White House meeting between Israeli and Lebanese officials. Live updates: Israel and Lebanon agree to extend ceasefire by 3 weeks, Trump says AP News.

item.title: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by 3 weeks following White House peace talks, Trump says
item.description: The Israel-Lebanon ceasefire has been extended by three weeks, US President Trump announced. The agreement follows a White House meeting between Israeli and Lebanese officials. Live Updates: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire extended by 3 weeks following White House peace talks, Trump says CBS News.

item.title: World Cup final tickets go on sale for $2.3m each
item.description: World Cup final tickets are on sale for $2.3m each. Tickets for this summer's football World Cup final have gone on sale for more than $2m each. World Cup final tickets go on sale for $2.3m each.

item.title: EU formally approves 90bn euro Ukraine loan and new sanctions on Russia
item.description: The EU approved a 90bn euro loan to Ukraine and new Russia sanctions. Ukrainian President Zelenskyy welcomes the development, urging that the first tranche be disbursed by May or June.

item.title: Will complex EU loan deal intensify conflict?
item.description: Ukraine received a multibillion-dollar loan from the EU. Ukraine gets multibillion-dollar loan; Russia rakes in oil revenue. Will complex EU loan deal intensify conflict?

item.title: Trump says he is keeping Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran
item.description: Donald Trump said he is keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran. Donald Trump said he is the one keeping the Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran. Trump says he is keeping Strait of Hormuz closed, not Iran.

item.title: Dollar Tug-of-War Tilts Toward Geopolitics. What Comes Next.
item.description: The dollar is influenced by geopolitics. Dollar Tug-of-War Tilts Toward Geopolitics. What Comes Next. Barron's.

item.title: US boards ship carrying Iran oil as Trump threatens mine-laying vessels
item.description: The US intercepted 33 vessels since imposing a blockade on ships coming to and from Iran. Central Command said it has intercepted 33 vessels since imposing a blockade on ships coming to and from Iran.

item.title: Afghans Who Helped U.S. Forces Say They’re Being Pushed Back to the Taliban
item.description: Afghan refugees who helped US forces may be sent to Congo. Once promised a move to the United States, Afghan refugees who helped U. S. forces say they face ‘bad or worse’ options: resettlement to Congo or returning home to live under the Taliban.

item.title: Airlines in Europe slash thousands of flights as Iran war cuts jet fuel supplies
item.description: European airlines are canceling tens of thousands of flights due to soaring jet fuel costs amid the Iran war. Energy authorities warn of a possible jet fuel shortage if supplies aren't replenished.

item.title: Trump To Meet Lebanon, Israel Envoys During Peace Talks
item.description: Trump will meet Lebanon and Israel envoys during peace talks after a 10-day truce was announced. The United States announced a 10-day truce shortly after the first meeting. Trump To Meet Lebanon, Israel Envoys During Peace Talks.

item.title: Meta To Layoff 10% Workforce Amid Heavy AI Investments: Report
item.description: Meta will lay off 10% of its workforce. Mark Zuckerberg makes a priority of delivering "superintelligence" in a costly AI race. Meta To Layoff 10% Workforce Amid Heavy AI Investments: Report.

item.title: Air Defence System Activated In Tehran Amid Reports Of Explosions
item.description: Tehran activated its air defense system after reports of explosions in several parts of the capital. The Mehr news agency confirmed the activation. Mehr news agency reported that the systems were activated in several parts of the capital.

item.title: Tanzania Report Blames ‘Outside Forces’ for Deadly Election Violence
item.description: Tanzania blames outside forces for election violence. The findings in a new report by a commission appointed by the government were at odds with details from human rights groups, which estimate that hundreds and possibly thousands of people were killed.

item.title: Touring Africa, Pope Leo Raised His Voice, but Didn’t Like the Echo
item.description: Pope Leo XIV made forthright comments on his Africa trip, but grew uncomfortable with the criticism's interpretation. On his recent trip abroad, Leo XIV made some of his most forthright comments since becoming pope last year, but grew uncomfortable at how that criticism was interpreted.
